Question: People in the US have longer paid vacations than people in which Western countries?

Answer:
generation: According to data from the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development (OECD), people in the United States have some of the shortest paid vacation time among Western countries. The OECD reports that the average number of paid vacation days per year in the US is around 10-14 days, which is shorter than many other Western countries such as Canada (20-25 days), Europe (20-25 days), and Japan (20-25 days).
